nodes.label: Windows Symlink Caveat for Vendored Skill

nodes.label: Next.js 16 ComponentMod.handler Architecture
nodes.rationale: All App Router pages dispatch through ComponentMod.handler(req,res,ctx) (base-server.js:1462); Turbopack registers handler as a lazy getter, so an undefined handler indicates a stale/mixed .next cache rather than a code bug.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 Turbopack Root Confusion
nodes.rationale: Turbopack walks up the directory tree for lockfiles to infer workspace root; a parent lockfile causes mixed-root compilation. Pinning turbopack.root to __dirname and clearing .next/ fixes it.

nodes.label: bun/bunx not on PATH in Windows Claude Code environment
bun/bunx not on PATH in Windows Claude Code Environment
nodes.rationale: Tool-spawned shells on Windows get a minimal, non-login environment that doesn't replay the user's shell profile, so bun's PATH addition is missing; invoke via full path to ~/.bun/bin instead.
nodes.norm_label: bun/bunx not on path in windows claude code environment

nodes.label: cred-grid CSS hardcodes column count to card count
cred-grid CSS Hardcodes Column Count to Card Count
nodes.rationale: .cred-grid's repeat(5,1fr) is hardcoded to the current card count in app/page.tsx; adding/removing a .cred card without updating N leaves a gap or squeezes cards.
nodes.norm_label: cred-grid css hardcodes column count to card count

nodes.label: DesignSync get_file truncates binary assets at 256 KB
DesignSync get_file Truncates Binary Assets at 256 KB
nodes.rationale: DesignSync's get_file MCP tool hard-caps base64 binary responses at 256 KB, silently corrupting larger images; the archived Claude Design project folder contains untruncated originals.
nodes.norm_label: designsync get_file truncates binary assets at 256 kb

nodes.label: graphify CLI not invokable via bun/bunx
graphify CLI Not Invokable via bun/bunx
nodes.rationale: graphify is not resolvable via bun/bunx PATH or bunx's package-runner fallback, unlike @playwright/cli which resolves by full package name; likely graphify isn't published under that bare name or its bin entry differs.
nodes.norm_label: graphify cli not invokable via bun/bunx

nodes.label: graphify --update Re-extraction Gotchas
nodes.rationale: AST extraction is content-invariant so pure copy edits need no re-extraction (and re-extracting a lone file without its import targets produces phantom full-path import IDs); build_merge replace-on-re-extract also drops ALL of a re-extracted file's existing edges, so hub files like _index.md/_log.md must be re-extracted with their COMPLETE link set or edges silently vanish.

nodes.label: next/font — axes + weight constraint for variable fonts
build_merge Replace-on-Re-extract Drops All File Edges
nodes.norm_label: build_merge replace-on-re-extract drops all file edges

nodes.label: next/font axes + weight Constraint for Variable Fonts
nodes.rationale: next/font/google throws a build error if axes is combined with an explicit weight array; weight must be omitted to get the full variable range when requesting a non-standard axis like opsz.
nodes.norm_label: next/font — axes + weight constraint for variable fonts

nodes.label: playwright-cli vs @playwright/test — two separate tools
nodes.norm_label: next/font axes + weight constraint for variable fonts

nodes.label: Casa Colina site duplicates marketing facts across sections
Casa Colina Site Duplicates Marketing Facts Across Sections
nodes.rationale: Marketing facts (phone, resident count, license tier) are restated verbatim in multiple unrelated markup locations rather than sourced once, so a single circled screenshot annotation understates true edit scope; the marquee strip additionally duplicates its own string twice for the scroll-loop animation.
nodes.norm_label: casa colina site duplicates marketing facts across sections

nodes.label: Turbopack CSS file-watch miss on programmatic write
nodes.rationale: Turbopack's OS file-notification watcher can silently miss programmatic writes to CSS files during dev, serving a stale compiled chunk; touching the file's mtime reliably forces reprocessing.
nodes.norm_label: turbopack css file-watch miss on programmatic write

nodes.rationale: Turbopack watches files via the OS file notification API (kqueue on macOS); programmatic writes sometimes don't emit a notification event, or the event fires before the write is fully flushed, leaving the watcher unaware of the change. `touch app/globals.css` updates mtime and reliably triggers reprocessing.
nodes.norm_label: turbopack css file-watch miss on programmatic write

nodes.label: Wiki Gotchas Lack Code-Side Back-References
nodes.rationale: graphify's own generated graph showed wiki gotcha articles have zero structural edges back to the source lines they describe, risking silent reintroduction of already-solved bugs during refactors since nothing in the code points a future editor at the constraint.

nodes.label: AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md shim pattern
nodes.rationale: Different AI coding agents read different context files (Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, Codex and others read AGENTS.md); maintaining both in full leads to drift, so AGENTS.md holds canonical content and CLAUDE.md is reduced to a one-line `@AGENTS.md` import shim.

nodes.label: Bug-driven TDD — red spec before fix
nodes.rationale: Writing a red Playwright spec asserting fixed behavior before any fix code forces a precise, executable definition of 'fixed', makes each green test proof rather than opinion, and creates a permanent regression guard so the bug cannot silently reintroduce itself.
nodes.norm_label: bug-driven tdd — red spec before fix

nodes.label: Azure DevOps MCP has no whoami tool — use core_get_identity_ids
nodes.norm_label: azure devops mcp has no whoami tool — use core_get_identity_ids

nodes.label: Azure DevOps Task fields reject certain values on state-transition updates
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Azure DevOps Task fields reject certain values on state-transition updates.md
nodes.rationale: ADO's Reason field only accepts values from the work item type's defined transition set, not arbitrary text (TF401320 on mismatch); RemainingWork treats an explicit zero as empty (InvalidNotEmpty), which the close-task validation rule rejects. Fix: omit Reason (ADO auto-fills a valid default) and leave RemainingWork at its prior value.
nodes.norm_label: azure devops task fields reject certain values on state-transition updates

nodes.label: Azure DevOps User Story Tasks panel renders as a checklist only with per-AC child Tasks
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Azure DevOps User Story Tasks panel renders as a checklist only with per-AC child Tasks.md
nodes.norm_label: azure devops user story tasks panel renders as a checklist only with per-ac child tasks

nodes.label: Azure DevOps multiline field format (HTML vs Markdown) varies per work item, not by type
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Azure DevOps multiline field format (HTML vs Markdown) varies per work item, not by type.md
nodes.rationale: Rendering format for multiline fields (Description, AcceptanceCriteria) is stored per-work-item in the response's multilineFieldsFormat map, not fixed by work item type or process template, so writing the wrong syntax silently renders wrong instead of erroring. A later session found a prior claim (backtick-wrapped angle brackets get stripped) was contradicted by contrary evidence — flagged as an unresolved conflict needing per-field verification.
nodes.norm_label: azure devops multiline field format (html vs markdown) varies per work item, not by type

nodes.label: Azure DevOps wit_update_work_item rejects mixed-field-type batches
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Azure DevOps wit_update_work_item rejects mixed-field-type batches.md
nodes.rationale: Combining a content-and-format multiline patch (AC + multilineFieldsFormat) with unrelated scalar fields (Priority, StoryPoints) in one wit_update_work_item call fails with a generic 'type changed without a value' error, reproduced identically across two separate stories; splitting into single-purpose calls succeeds. Discovered empirically — the tool schema gives no hint why.
nodes.norm_label: azure devops wit_update_work_item rejects mixed-field-type batches

nodes.label: Vercel CLI first deploy auto-connects GitHub
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Vercel CLI first deploy auto-connects GitHub.md
nodes.rationale: Vercel CLI v54+ detects a GitHub-origin git remote and auto-connects the repo to the new Vercel project during the first `vercel --prod --yes` deploy, provided the Vercel GitHub App is already installed on the org/account — no separate `vercel git connect` command needed.
nodes.norm_label: vercel cli first deploy auto-connects github

nodes.label: Vercel GitHub integration auto-deploys production on merge to main
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Vercel GitHub integration auto-deploys production on merge to main.md
nodes.norm_label: vercel github integration auto-deploys production on merge to main

nodes.label: Vercel bun auto-detection
nodes.rationale: Presence of bun.lock (and absence of package-lock.json/pnpm-lock.yaml) is the sole signal Vercel's build system uses to select bun for install/build (`bun install` then `bun run build`), without needing a vercel.json override.

nodes.label: Vercel domain alias API vs project domains API
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Vercel domain alias vs project domains API.md
nodes.norm_label: vercel domain alias api vs project domains api

nodes.label: Windows git worktree remove fails due to own shell cwd
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/Windows git worktree remove fails due to own shell cwd.md
nodes.rationale: Two distinct causes: (1) a leftover next dev/Turbopack node.exe process holds file locks in .next/ and node_modules/.bin; (2) the agent's own persistent shell still has cwd inside the worktree, which holds a Windows directory handle open and blocks delete/rename even with zero other processes running.
nodes.norm_label: windows git worktree remove fails due to own shell cwd

nodes.label: graphify Scope for casacolinacare-v2
nodes.rationale: A full-repo scan hits ~5.65M words dominated by .claude/ session logs and claude_design/ assets, neither adding real code or doc signal; scoping to app/, tests/, wiki/, and root config via .graphifyignore (honored by the incremental --update path) keeps updates clean. An incomplete ignore list leaked ~120 files (mostly .claude/data/sessions and .agents/skills docs) into a --update scan before .claude/, .agents/, and scratchpad/ were added.

nodes.label: jx-pm:impl-story-plan's worktree isolation can't see changes already staged in the main tree
nodes.source_file: wiki/platforms/jx-pm impl-story-plan's worktree isolation can't see changes already staged in the main tree.md
nodes.norm_label: jx-pm:impl-story-plan's worktree isolation can't see changes already staged in the main tree

nodes.label: core_get_identity_ids (Azure DevOps MCP tool)
nodes.rationale: A fresh worktree checks out from HEAD only, so it has no visibility into the main tree's staged-but-uncommitted git index state, nor into completely untracked files (e.g. a referenced image asset never `git add`-ed) — `git worktree add` only materializes committed, tracked content.
nodes.norm_label: jx-pm:impl-story-plan's worktree isolation can't see changes already staged in the main tree
